page: Amazon Web Services r8ib.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation and minimized virtualization overhead. It does not include local storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8ib.24xlarge offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The absence of local storage and GPU hardware positions this instance specifically for workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, big data analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz, the instance provides 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with dedicated CPU allocation. It features 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB RAM-to-vCPU ratio. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage. This hardware profile is engineered for memory-bound applications, including in-memory databases, large-scale caching systems, and enterprise data processing workloads that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local NVMe drives or specialized graphics hardware.
